title: mentha tristis
intro: Mentha tristis, also known as wild bergamot, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to North America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues and promoting relaxation. In herbal preparations, Mentha tristis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to soothe anxiety, improve sleep, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
